reviewer.evidence: candidate.c is HEAD minus the two register-asm pins; every construct passes the six-test checklist. Named-intermediate t/mask is SOTN-sanctioned. No forbidden family constructs introduced.

remaining_gap.summary: Tail-only v0/v1 register-rotation: target uses v0 for the three arg0[] loads and v1 for the mask (lui/ori 0xFF8080) with `lui $v1,0xFF` interleaved between the 2nd lw and 2nd sw; unpinned GCC swaps both (loads->v1, mask->v0) on the same schedule. Masked Levenshtein = 9 on every pure-C form explored across this function and both siblings (80061658, 80061710). Schedule is byte-identical; this is purely the GCC 2.7.2 local-alloc preference flip discussed in memory/project/register-alloc-deep-dive.md.
remaining_gap.rules_required_for_full_match: Two register-asm pins (t->$2, mask->$3); no regfix rules. Pure-C reordering does not reproduce the rotation (sibling 80061658, 80061710, and this function's 8-form sweep all confirm).
